raw_results.query: best practices autonomous LLM agent memory management
new methods for LLM agent orchestration and multi-agent coordination

suggestions: Self-Reflection and In-Loop Feedback**: Agents improve at inference time via Reflexion (iterative reflection on failures), Self-Refine (prompt-level refinement), and trained self-correction like RISE, STaR, SELF, or STaSC. Self-rewarding models score their own outputs for RL training, while self-consistency aggregates multiple reasoning chains for reliability.[1]

Self-Generated Data and Curricula**: Agents create their own training examples, problems, or demonstrations, filtering for quality to drive self-training and avoid poor signals. This extends to peer learning among agents sharing strategies.[1][2]

Self-Adapting Models**: Techniques like SEAL generate self-edit instructions (e.g., "prefer answer type X for certain patterns"), converting them into fine-tuning data using downstream performance as reward.[1]
